summary: The diff implements most requirements well: backend Motoko data store with additives, parsing function, frontend scan page with textarea, results cards with summary banner, encyclopedia page with search/filter/detail view, green/white health-wellness theme, and logo in header. One significant acceptance criterion is uncertain — the backend is truncated so we cannot confirm at least 80 additives are stored. The textarea label phrasing is partially met via placeholder text. All other core requirements appear implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend stores at least 80 well-known additives and preservatives
missing_requirements.reason: The backend file is truncated after only ~8 additives are visible in the diff. There is no evidence the full list reaches 80+ entries. Cannot confirm this AC is met.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity authentication integration with login/logout and identity management
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention user accounts, authentication, or Internet Identity. The diff includes a full InternetIdentityProvider, useInternetIdentity hook, and actor authentication wiring, which was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Automated concern level classification via keyword matching on healthEffects text
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-6 requests color coding for safety/concern levels but does not specify an automated keyword-based classification algorithm. The implementation adds a getConcernLevel() utility that infers concern from health effect text keywords, going beyond what was specified.
